description: The repo_sync recovery task is reinitialized as a standalone harness with the missing service-module baseline clearly separated from older dormant gateway debt.
steps: Open PLAN.json and verify it only contains repo_sync recovery work rather than the completed coding-task cleanup plan.
Open PROGRESS.md and verify the new reboot entry records the missing module baseline, the scoped goal, and what is intentionally out of scope.
Confirm the harness scope is limited to repo_sync service recovery instead of also reviving missing app.prompts or app.runtime modules.

description: The nanobot.repo_sync package exports a concrete RepoSyncWatcher service module again so the repo-wide test collector can import it.
steps: Import nanobot.repo_sync.service in a Python shell.
Verify RepoSyncWatcher resolves without ModuleNotFoundError.
Confirm the package layout includes source files rather than only stale __pycache__ artifacts.

description: RepoSyncWatcher accepts the gateway's current constructor contract, including sync-runner injection and repo-sync policy fields.
steps: Instantiate RepoSyncWatcher with the same keyword arguments used by the dormant gateway path.
Verify the watcher stores the repo path, branch, remotes, timing controls, and sync runner without raising.
Confirm the watcher can be built both with a custom sync_runner and with the default sync helper.

description: RepoSyncWatcher start() is idempotent and runs an immediate sync when run_on_start is enabled.
steps: Start a watcher configured with run_on_start=True and an injected fake sync runner.
Verify the runner is called once immediately after startup.
Call start() again and confirm no duplicate immediate sync is scheduled.

description: RepoSyncWatcher can poll on a short interval when sync_hour disables nightly scheduling.
steps: Start a watcher with sync_hour set outside the nightly range and a short interval.
Let it run briefly under asyncio.
Verify the injected sync runner is invoked multiple times before stop() is called.

description: RepoSyncWatcher stop() cancels the background loop cleanly without leaving a live task reference behind.
steps: Start a watcher and then call stop().
Verify the background task is cancelled cleanly and the watcher no longer reports itself as running.
Confirm repeated stop() calls remain harmless.

description: trigger_now() serializes concurrent sync attempts through an async lock so overlapping polls cannot run the sync runner twice at once.
steps: Call trigger_now() concurrently from two coroutines against a watcher with a slow fake runner.
Verify the calls complete without overlapping runner execution.
Confirm the watcher still records both trigger requests sequentially.

description: The default sync_fork_once helper provides a safe baseline implementation that reports validation failures and no-op sync states without crashing the watcher.
steps: Invoke the default sync helper with a missing repo path.
Verify it returns a human-readable failed status instead of raising uncaught exceptions.
Confirm RepoSyncWatcher can use the default helper without requiring a custom test stub.

description: Focused tests cover the restored watcher lifecycle and synchronization behavior without depending on dormant gateway imports.
steps: Run tests/test_repo_sync_service.py.
Verify the suite covers immediate startup, interval polling, and idempotent start behavior.
Confirm any new watcher tests exercise serialization or stop semantics directly through the service module.

description: Repo-wide pytest no longer stops at tests/test_repo_sync_service.py import collection because the service module exists again.
steps: Run the repository's standard pytest command.
Verify test collection proceeds past tests/test_repo_sync_service.py without ModuleNotFoundError.
Record any remaining unrelated failures in PROGRESS.md if additional baseline issues surface.

description: PROGRESS.md captures the repo_sync recovery decisions so future sessions know the service layer was intentionally restored as a thin watcher rather than a full gateway revival.
steps: Open the newest PROGRESS.md entry.
Verify it records the source of truth for the missing-module failure and the chosen thin-watcher recovery strategy.
Confirm the log distinguishes repo_sync recovery from the still-dormant app.prompts and app.runtime gaps.
